data.title: Nvidia-Backed AI Company Profound Partners with CRS Credit API to Boost AI Search By 20x
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA)-backed AI company Profound has partnered with CRS Credit API to enhance AI search capabilities. The collaboration has reportedly produced a 20× increase in AI search visibility and driven over 15% growth in CRS's AI-driven pipeline. The partnership comes as CRS seeks to adapt to "Answer Engines" such as ChatGPT and Grok AI, with Profound providing a system to enhance AI-driven content and search visibility.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Profound and CRS Build AI Search Growth Engine

In a LinkedIn post on Feb. 14, co-founder James Cadwallader announced that Profound has officially teamed up with CRS Credit API, an all-in-one platform for credit data, fraud detection, and compliance solutions. Cadwallader said the collaboration was designed to help CRS navigate AI-driven Answer Engines. The partnership comes after CRS experienced a slowdown in inbound growth from paid advertising as demand shifted toward AI-driven search, prompting it to seek Profound's support.

As a solution, both private companies have implemented a repeatable content system using Profound's Answer Engine Insights to produce high-impact opportunities in AI-driven search. The strategy resulted in a 20× increase in AI search visibility and over 15% growth in CRS's pipeline from AI-driven traffic. Additionally, weekly traffic rose by 8%, attributed to citations from large language models (LLMs).

Ronak Patel, Head of Marketing at CRS Credit API, said the new system goes beyond monitoring dashboards, offering strategic guidance to optimize content for evolving Answer Engines and LLMs. Meanwhile, Cadwallader noted that the partnership enhances discoverability in a "zero-click internet," where users find answers directly in search results without clicking through to websites.

AI Search Becomes a Key Competitive Channel

CRS Credit API aims to leverage Profound's platform to tie AI visibility metrics directly to business outcomes, including MQLs, conversion rates, and revenue. Patel acknowledged that AI search was a new channel for the company. As a result, they needed Profound's expert guidance to maximize visibility and help more prospects find them. The collaboration created a feedback loop between Profound's tools and CRS's content distribution, enabling them to outperform competitors by a wide margin.

Patel also highlighted the partnership's strategic advantage, noting that Profound's Answer Engine Insights and FAQ Agent helped optimize key product and blog pages for LLMs. The alliance allowed CRS to scale AI-driven search while maintaining rapid responsiveness to changes in traffic and content needs.

Is Nvidia a Strong Buy Now? 

Nvidia has received a "Strong Buy" consensus rating from TipRanks analysts. The NVDA stock is currently trading at $182 per share, with an upside potential of more than 42%. The average price target for this stock is $260.38, with a high forecast of $352. 

To get more updates and analysts' ratings on stocks, explore the TipRanks Stock comparison center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: QuEra Computing Issues Call for Researchers to Run Projects on its Quantum Processors
data.description:
QuEra Computing, the Boston-based neutral-atom quantum pioneer, has proposed a call for global researchers to access and run projects on its Quantum processors through its 2026 Quantum Computing Access at NERSC (QCAN) program. The initiative, run by the U.S. Department of Energy's National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC), is designed to advance hybrid quantum–classical research.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

QuEra Opens Aquila and Gemini Systems to Researchers 

In a recent LinkedIn post, QuEra Computing said the proposed 2026 call allows selected researchers to run projects on its Aquila (analog) and Gemini (gate-based) neutral-atom quantum processors. 

The program is explicitly designed to move beyond exploratory demonstrations, focusing instead on hardware-aware workflows integrated with high-performance computing (HPC) environments. The structure aligns with the U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science priorities across quantum computing, materials science, chemistry, energy systems, and fundamental physics. 

According to the outlined framework, up to six projects will be selected across the Aquila and Gemini platforms. The initiative will run in two stages. Stage A, scheduled for April through June 2026, will focus on workflow development, simulation, and initial hardware runs on Aquila. Subsequently, Stage B, which will run from July through Dec. 2026, will provide full hardware allocations to teams that successfully advance from the first phase.

QuEra Outlines Hardware Allocations and Evaluation Criteria

Under the program's allocation model, Aquila will provide up to approximately 37.5 total QPU-hours, operating at roughly three shots per second. Gemini, on the other hand, will offer up to 10 QPU-hours at around one shot per second, following a simulation-first validation process to ensure readiness before hardware execution. All results generated through the program must be open and publishable, reinforcing its scientific research orientation, as stated by QuEra Computing.

The proposals, which are due Feb.28, will be evaluated based on technical feasibility, statistical rigor, clearly defined success metrics, and demonstrated full-time research commitment. The evaluation criteria reflect what QuEra Computing described as "growing maturity" in the field, treating quantum processors as scientific instruments integrated with classical HPC systems rather than experimental novelties.

Is QuEra a Private Company? 

QuEra Computing is a privately held company, meaning only accredited and institutional investors may invest in its shares before it goes public. For more information on QuEra Computing and other private companies in the quantum and tech space, visit TipRanks' private company center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: "Agentic Defense Push" Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Acquires Koi to Boost Security for AI Workflows
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (PANW) is expanding its cybersecurity reach with the acquisition of Koi, an Israeli startup pioneering Agentic Endpoint Security. The deal, estimated at $400 million according to Israeli business outlet Globes, aims to help companies gain better visibility into and protection over the rapidly growing ecosystem of AI‑native tools now used in everyday work.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Koi was founded in 2024 by veterans of Israel's elite Unit 8200. Its technology focuses on protecting systems from autonomous AI agents, tools that can read, write, and move data with deep system access, often bypassing traditional security controls.

Palo Alto Networks said the acquisition will help close a growing security gap created by these AI-powered tools.

Strengthening Prisma AIRS and Cortex XDR

Once the deal closes, Koi's Agentic Endpoint Security technology will be integrated into Palo Alto Networks' Prisma AIRS, the company's AI security platform. The addition will broaden protection across AI-driven operations and give customers deeper visibility into how agents interact with data and systems.

Koi's capabilities will also enhance Cortex XDR, PANW's endpoint security solution, improving detection, policy, and malware prevention across the AI attack surface. The goal is to allow organizations to deploy AI agents with confidence rather than uncertainty.

Is PANW a Good Stock to Buy Today?

Turning to Wall Street, PANW stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 26 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $223.68, the average Palo Alto Networks stock price target implies a 37.72% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: "The Solar Opportunity is Underestimated": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Slides as Buffalo Workforce Ramping Up
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) may not be an electric vehicle giant much longer. It has made a lot of strides to diversify of late, and one of the big new things Tesla is pushing into is solar panels. In fact, reports suggest that Tesla's Buffalo workforce is getting some serious augmentation in the face of a potentially growing opportunity. Investors proved skeptical, though, judging by the nearly 2.5% drop in share prices in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla wants to roll out 100 gigawatts' worth of solar panels annually, reports note, a goal that is by any stretch ambitious. Tesla first got into solar when it acquired Solar City, but was only set to produce a bare hundredth of that number, just one gigawatt annually. But Tesla has been working, and recently revealed it has a new solar panel with not only more attractive looks, but also better performance in "partially shaded areas."

Tesla's own Elon Musk noted, "The solar opportunity is underestimated. We're gonna work towards getting 100 gigawatts a year of solar cell production, integrating across the entire supply chain, from raw materials all the way to finished solar panels." It would not be the first time a publicly-traded company went into solar panels, but few of them have done that well.

Kicking Tesla While It's Down

Many hope that Musk is right here, because the cars certainly have seen better days as far as sales go. A new story is going viral through Chinese state media about a Tesla Model Y that, somehow, managed to lose all power on the highway despite the fact it had significant range left to its battery, according to the cabin display.

The fact that Chinese media is state-controlled suggests that this story was deliberately amplified, reports note. This suggests a mood shift about Tesla in the mind of the Chinese government. Given how many of Tesla's competitors in the electric vehicle space are Chinese in nature, it is not outside the realm of possibility that the Chinese government may be taking a minor incident and using it as a goad to whip Tesla out of the market.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.88% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 1.9% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: IBM Stock Slides despite Strong Backing from Five-Star Analyst
data.description:
During IBM's (IBM) latest earnings call, management said that it expects to generate $15.7 billion in free cash flow in 2026. Analysts at Evercore believe that the target is not only realistic but could actually be surpassed. In a note to clients, five-star analyst Amit Daryanani said that the guidance appears conservative based on current trends. Starting from last year's $14.7 billion in free cash flow, they see several clear drivers that could push the total higher.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

More specifically, Evercore estimates that net income growth could add about $800 million to $900 million, while deferred revenue from growing software sales may contribute another $200 million to $300 million. On top of that, incremental stock-based compensation tied to software growth could add an additional $200 million to $300 million, since it is not added back in IBM's adjusted net income and therefore supports free cash flow.

However, these positives would be partially offset by roughly $200 million in higher capital expenditures and another $200 million in increased cash taxes. Nevertheless, even after accounting for those expenses, Evercore's calculations still indicate that free cash flow will exceed $15.7 billion in 2026. The firm also noted that interest costs related to IBM's planned $11 billion acquisition of Confluent (CFLT) have already been factored into year-over-year net income comparisons. As a result, Evercore kept its Buy rating on IBM and maintained a $345 price target.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 13 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $337.53 per share implies 30.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: "Tariff Math" Hits Coffee Consumers, Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Shrugs it Off
data.description:
Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) has long had its detractors, pointing out the high-priced nature of Starbucks products. And while many shrugged these off, as evidenced by Starbucks' quarterly sales figures, the idea that, perhaps, a less-expensive treat could be had elsewhere was hard to buck. Reports note that "tariff math" is starting to hit coffee buyers, and impact sales. But Starbucks investors brushed this off, and Starbucks shares gained modestly in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Coffee prices have been on the rise for the last several years, and coffee buffs are not taking the tide of price hikes lightly. Many are cutting out Starbucks runs in response, switching to home-brewed coffee instead. A Consumer Price Index study revealed that coffee prices were up 18.3% in January, when measured against January 2025. The numbers are even worse going back five years; the difference between January 2026 and January 2021, reports note, is 47%. Increased tariffs have prompted customers to perform "tariff math" to discover how deep the problem goes.

While coffee consumption overall is holding stable, there are signs that the market is starting to fracture, splitting away from higher-end beverages in favor of simpler choices. Sharon Cooksie from Greensboro, North Carolina, perhaps said it best: "I can buy a bag of coffee for $6? It was like I had just discovered another world. The multiverse opened up to me in the coffee aisle of Publix."

The Boba Bubble

One of those alternatives for some is boba tea, and indeed, Starbucks had a hand in getting one bubble tea chain, Gong Cha, off the ground. Gong Cha's founder, Martin Berry, noted that in the early days, he would open Gong Cha locations next to Starbucks outlets where he could. This gave him access to some of Starbucks' spillover business, and let the store take off.

Given that Gong Cha now has around 2,200 stores in 33 countries, the plan must have worked, and worked quite well. It represents another problem for Starbucks, though, in that it is another competitor in a market that is seeing stagnant growth by some reports. We have already seen a range of competitors step in to get a piece of Starbucks' market, but that market is not growing much, if at all.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 15 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 17.02% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $100.59 per share implies 6.07% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock (LLY) Rises on Plan to Invest $1 Billion in India
data.description:
The stock of Eli Lilly (LLY) is up about 1% after the pharmaceutical company announced plans to invest $1 billion in India as part of its global expansion.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, Eli Lilly is allocating $1 billion to bolster its contract manufacturing operations across India. The company said in a news release that it plans to leverage India as a manufacturing hub to serve international markets, particularly in Asia.

Beyond growing its manufacturing capabilities, Eli Lilly also intends to introduce additional products to the Indian market, including its bestselling Alzheimer's medication Donanemab. India is rapidly becoming a major center for prescription drug production due to its access to Asia and the Middle East.

Eli Lilly's Investments

Eli Lilly has been ramping up its global investments as it markets and sells its blockbuster weight-loss drug Zepbound around the world. Since 2020, Eli Lilly has committed over $50 billion to expanding its U.S. manufacturing footprint, driven by surging demand for its obesity medication.

Previously, Eli Lilly announced a $1 billion investment to expand its manufacturing capabilities in Hyderabad, India. With the company planning to launch a pill version of its weight-loss medication this spring, Eli Lilly's manufacturing investments are expected to increase throughout 2026.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Software Stocks Are Plummeting… Are Their Forward Estimates Next?
data.description:
Artificial intelligence is shaking up the software industry. Indeed, lightning-fast advances from companies like OpenAI, Google (GOOGL), and Anthropic are leading to serious worries about how traditional software businesses will compete. As a result, former high-fliers such as Workday (WDAY) and Salesforce (CRM) are seeing sharp declines in their stock prices. Even so, many Wall Street analysts have been slow to adjust their earnings forecasts, which suggests that they may not fully appreciate the long-term disruption risk.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That gap becomes clear when looking at the numbers. According to Goldman Sachs (GS) strategist Ben Snider, software stocks tracked by the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) have fallen about 24% over the past three months. However, during that same period, two-year forward earnings estimates for the group have actually increased by 5%. Likewise, other industries seen as vulnerable to AI, including insurance brokers and advertising companies, have also received positive revisions to their 2026 earnings estimates.

As a result, some strategists believe that the gap will eventually close, and not in a good way. This is because historically, price action often reflects underlying risks before analysts adjust their forecasts. Software stocks that once traded at price-to-earnings multiples near 35 times are now below 20 times, according to Innovator Capital Management's Tim Urbanowicz, who spoke to Yahoo Finance. He also went on to say that this sell-off highlights the "dark side" of AI and serves as a warning that disruption could spread to other sectors as well.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
